tables.name: persons
tables.description: Pipedrive-style person candidates from Salesforce Contact. Join account on AccountId for organization name when needed.

columns.name: sf_contact_id
columns.description: Salesforce Contact Id (preserve for traceability and idempotent load later).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Full display name — trim; combine FirstName and LastName with a single space.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Primary email; lowercased and trimmed (use Email if present).

columns.name: phone
columns.description: Preferred phone — Phone or MobilePhone.

columns.name: org_name
columns.description: Account name looked up via Contact.AccountId = Account.Id (staging table account).

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map from OwnerId if you can coerce to integer, otherwise null.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Contact.CreatedDate if available.

tables.name: organizations
tables.description: Pipedrive-style organization candidates from Salesforce Account.

columns.name: sf_account_id
columns.description: Salesforce Account Id (preserve for traceability).

columns.name: name
columns.description: Account Name; trimmed.

columns.name: address
columns.description: Billing address summary — concatenate BillingStreet, BillingCity, BillingState, BillingPostalCode, BillingCountry with commas/spaces.

columns.name: owner_id
columns.description: Placeholder for Pipedrive owner; map OwnerId when possible.

columns.name: add_time
columns.description: Creation time from Account.CreatedDate if present.

column_notes.explanation: Direct mapping from contact.id to maintain traceability.
Concatenates first_name and last_name with a space, then trims whitespace.
Lowercased and trimmed version of contact.email.
Uses primary phone if available, falling back to mobile_phone via COALESCE.
Fetched from account.name via the join on account_id.
Attempts to cast owner_id to integer using regex validation; returns NULL if not numeric.

tables.reasoning: I performed a LEFT JOIN between contacts and accounts to enrich person records with organization names. I implemented a regex-based check for the owner_id integer coercion to prevent casting errors in PostgreSQL, and used CONCAT_WS and TRIM for clean name and email formatting.

I used CONCAT_WS with NULLIF to create a clean, comma-separated address string without trailing or double commas. I also implemented a regex guard for owner_id to safely handle the requirement of integer coercion in PostgreSQL without using non-standard TRY_CAST.

tables.reasoning: The model joins the Contact and Account source tables to enrich person records with organization details. It uses PostgreSQL regex validation for safe integer conversion of the owner_id to meet the target specification requirements.

The model uses PostgreSQL array functions for robust address concatenation that ignores null/empty fields. It also implements a regex-based numeric check for the owner_id to satisfy the requirement of mapping to integer only where possible, preventing runtime casting errors from alphanumeric Salesforce IDs.

tables.reasoning:
This mapping plan transforms Salesforce CRM data into a Pipedrive-style schema. The 'persons' table is created by joining 'src_01kvb56edsvfkc8jxka4kvpmv5_raw.contact' with 'src_01kvb56edsvfkc8jxka4kvpmv5_raw.account' on contact.account_id = account.id. The 'organizations' table is derived directly from the 'account' source. String normalization (trimming/lowercasing) and null-safe concatenation are applied globally.
The model implements a LEFT JOIN to preserve all contacts even without associated accounts. It uses PostgreSQL-specific regex ('~') for safe integer casting of Salesforce IDs and CONCAT_WS for robust name concatenation handling NULL values.

This mapping plan transforms Salesforce CRM data into a Pipedrive-style schema. The 'persons' table is created by joining 'src_01kvb56edsvfkc8jxka4kvpmv5_raw.contact' with 'src_01kvb56edsvfkc8jxka4kvpmv5_raw.account' on contact.account_id = account.id. The 'organizations' table is derived directly from the 'account' source. String normalization (trimming/lowercasing) and null-safe concatenation are applied globally.
Used CONCAT_WS for a robust, null-safe address construction and implemented a regex-based conditional cast to handle the conversion of Salesforce's alphanumeric owner IDs into the target integer type safely.

tables.reasoning:
judge: Candidate 0 provides the cleanest and most robust implementation. It uses CONCAT_WS to handle potential NULL values in names gracefully (avoiding trailing/leading spaces or resulting NULLs), correctly implements the conditional integer cast for owner_id using PostgreSQL-compatible regex, and explicitly selects required columns rather than using SELECT *, which is better practice.
The model performs a LEFT JOIN between contacts and accounts to enrich contact data with organization names. I used a regex check (~) before casting owner_id to integer to comply with the target type while avoiding PostgreSQL cast errors on alphanumeric strings, and applied standard normalization (LOWER/TRIM) as requested.

judge: Candidate 0 is the most robust implementation. It uses `NULLIF(TRIM(...), '')` within `CONCAT_WS`, which correctly prevents empty or whitespace-only strings from creating trailing or double commas in the address field. It also includes a `WHERE id IS NOT NULL` clause to satisfy the target schema's NOT NULL constraint on `sf_account_id`.
I used CONCAT_WS with NULLIF to build a clean, comma-delimited address string. To safely convert the alphanumeric owner_id to an integer in PostgreSQL without using unsupported functions, I implemented a regex check.
